slightly afraid I fucked something up with the yolo, but a different fuckup got rectified - I can simply use my larva predictions as intervention and use the natural noisyness of the generation process as a learning indicator/intervention for training and I don't even need confirmation - future problem solved. house shit is a bit weird, but I think I can find a house in a bad neighbourhood - what are the odds it doesn't become better? check if a neighbourhood *is* getting better. strill a bit iffy though. I should be more attentive to my time AND DRIVING TEST PREPARATION FUCK MAN

I'm not stable but also ont uinstable; I'm not impaled but impressed. I hope I'm making good decisions but for now it is what it is., after talking about my problem I feel less stressed. I hope I get the opportunity to do that more often, but right now I'm quite tired so time for sleep now. made some mistakes so things could be better but they could also be a whole lot worse.

ik voel me ondanks dat ik de dingen met robert aangegeven nog steeds neit super chill erbij, ik heb dingen te zacht en daardoor te inadequaat aangepakt. ik heb de meeste dingen enigszins op de rails op dit na. then again het was niet 100% aan mij om er op te letten, uitgekomen op 63.25 op basis van een geometric average of 50% & 80%, I think I can live with that and that is what I'll aim to update for. signprop works when error margins are noisy, i.e. not mainly due to you. in a sense I've been trained to not be calibrated to feedback well. I need to make it a point to calibrate it properly, that will help me. bad past me, good present me, but in all fairness past me did the best he could, it's not like he diddn't want things to go well.

it's been a while; the work project of vertel is going reasonable - I feel like im a but overburdened though but that's mainly due to misunderstanding/being too afraid to have throw away stuff I guess. buying a house is fucking weird though. time goes by quickly, hope I make the right calls.
I keep waking up tired, don't know why. easy, I ate like shit and that's causing it, duh I should probably make a bigger point to change that.
huisie kopuuuh

It's kind of interesting when you're faced with a choice in life and life shouws you how options might play out by showing you people with different approaches on *that* issue with the same name.

I guess, as a robert, you can either quit the bullshit and end up relatively rich or end up more entangled in relationships. you either take care of the little things or lose sigh of them and manage your responsiveness or numb it out.

learn to ascribe value and choose accordingly. don't do things that have power over you, that pull you in. striving to be present is something very different from being present. if you're running after things you're also not being present. it is like driving in a care. you have to be looking ahead. continuously or stop.

if you are not dreaming you are messing up by exploring too little.
sometimes getting things in one place is difficult; it's a sign you're subservient to your environment. otherwise they would always be where you would want them.

Yesterday I was too tired, did too much, gotta make sure I'm prepared for the week again, slowly... bit worried how I'll manage everything though, time flies by too fast, dislike how I'll convince myself of things that will not happen.

still, we'll see how things go.

after the house is sorted I will be building my workshop/improved office things can start fucking taking off.

I notice I often come back to this when I have a new feature I want to add or to get some kind of confirmation bias; I think what this thing needs is a feedback loop. that said, I feel very tired and have for a couple days now.

I think what I'm missing is a view of where I am going.

That's a fundamentally different question. You have a rearview mirror (logs of where you've been), but no dashboard showing trajectory or destination.

What would "where I am going" look like practically?

Option A: Trend projection
Show whether your scores are trending up/down/stable over time. "Your negative affect has increased 15% over the last month" → signals something needs to change.

Option B: Goals/intentions tracking
Each entry: "What do I want to work on?" Then later entries show: "Last time you said you wanted to [X]. How's that going?" Accountability loop.

Option C: Future-self prompts
"Based on your patterns, if nothing changes, in 3 months you'll likely feel [X]." Forces you to decide if that's acceptable.

Option D: Intervention effectiveness
Track: "When I [exercise/eat well/sleep], my scores change by [X]." Shows what actually moves the needle vs. what you think works.

Option E: Simple trajectory question
Just add to each entry: "Am I moving toward or away from where I want to be?" Track that over time. Binary, honest, actionable.

Which of these (or something else) matches what "where I am going" means to you?

Claude Sonnet 4.5 • 1x


I think the answer lies somewhere between D & E, but I have no clue where I want to be going, right now I just want to not be tired; maybe what I need is a LLM integration so that I can ask it what helped before, specifically given my logged data.

That makes sense. You want to query your past self - "When was I not tired? What was different then?" - without manually re-reading everything.

An LLM integration would let you ask:

"Show me entries where I felt energetic"
"What patterns appear before I feel overwhelmed at work?"
"When did I last feel good about progress?"
"Compare my state now vs. when I bought the house"
Given that it's a self-contained HTML file, the practical approach would be:

Add an "Ask about my data" section
Include your entries as context in a prompt to an LLM API (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)
Let you ask natural language questions
Get answers grounded in your actual logged experiences
This fits your usage pattern - you come back when you're processing something, and now you could actually reflect by interrogating your own data rather than just adding to it.

Want me to add this? Would need:

API key input (stored locally)
Simple chat interface
System prompt that understands PANAS scores + your log format
Sends your full entry history as context with each question
Should take ~30-45 min to implement. Worth trying?
